turns.user_message: I have finally accepted that my internal spark is completely gone. It has been dragging on for months now, and I just keep pretending everything is fine. I tried to listen to my favorite playlist yesterday, but the songs just sounded like background noise. I sat there waiting for a single familiar feeling to hit me, but my chest stayed completely empty. It is strange how quiet everything feels when nothing actually registers anymore.

I keep telling myself that this dullness is just a temporary phase, but the weeks keep stacking up without any change. Every single morning starts with the exact same heavy routine, and I just move through it on pure habit. I used to look forward to simple little moments, but now they all blur together into one long stretch of gray. I catch myself staring at the wall and wondering why my brain refuses to light up. It feels like I am trapped behind a thick pane of glass while life happens somewhere else.

I have completely surrendered to the fact that my capacity for joy has just evaporated into thin air. Nothing I do seems to reach me anymore, and I just exist in this endless loop of mechanical repetition. I try to force a reaction out of myself by doing things that should matter, but it all just washes over me like water on stone. The silence inside my own head is so loud that it drowns out everything else around me. I am just a hollow shell going through the motions, waiting for a feeling that will never show up.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
